rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular oxyselenenylation of N-allylureas with diaryl diselenides to access selenomethyl-substituted cyclic isoureas (5- to 7-membered) via C-O/C-Se bond formation
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of diphenyl diselenide (PhSeSePh) generates the electrophilic phenylselenenyl cation (PhSe+); cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: phenylselenenyl cation (PhSe+) generated by anodic SET on PhSeSePh
seleniranium/thiiranium-like ion (B) from PhSe+ addition across the allyl C=C of the N-allylurea
selenomethyl-substituted cyclic isourea product 3a after intramolecular O-attack and deprotonation

electron_flow: At the anode, PhSeSePh loses two electrons to give 2 equiv of PhSe+; PhSe+ adds to the alkene of the N-allylurea forming a seleniranium-type cation B, which is opened by intramolecular nucleophilic attack of the urea oxygen, followed by loss of H+. The cathode reduces 2H+ to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction proceeded smoothly with 1 equiv TEMPO giving 3a in 80% yield, indicating no free radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction proceeded smoothly with 1 equiv BHT giving 3a in 79% yield, no inhibition

probe_reagents.name: phenyl hypochloroselenoite (PhSeCl, 5a)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction of 1a with PhSeCl under standard conditions without electrolysis afforded 3a in 13% yield, supporting involvement of an electrophilic PhSe+ species

notes: CV shows PhSeSePh (2a) oxidizes at ~1.0 V vs Ag/AgCl while 1a oxidizes at ~1.3 V, confirming the diselenide (not the urea/alkene) is the species oxidized at the anode. Authors initially describe the pathway as a 'cationic radical mechanism' based on TEMPO/BHT not inhibiting and the PhSeCl experiment, but the proposed cycle (Figure 3) is fundamentally a two-electron electrophilic selenocyclization via PhSe+ (cation-initiated), with no discrete C-centered radical invoked.
